NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. LORD, my heart is not haughty, Nor my eyes lofty. Neither do I concern myself with great matters, Nor with things too profound for me.
NKJV.2: Surely I have calmed and quieted my soul, Like a weaned child with his mother; Like a weaned child is my soul within me.
NKJV.3: O Israel, hope in the LORD From this time forth and forever.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S> of David<S>1732</S>. LORD<S>3068</S>, my heart<S>3820</S> is not haughty<S>1361</S>, nor mine eyes<S>5869</S> lofty<S>7311</S>: neither do I exercise<S>1980</S> myself in great matters<S>1419</S>, or in things too high<S>6381</S> for me. <sup>exercise: Heb. walk</sup> <sup>high: Heb. wonderful</sup>
KJV.2: Surely I have behaved<S>7737</S> and quieted<S>1826</S> myself<S>5315</S>, as a child that is weaned<S>1580</S> of his mother<S>517</S>: my soul<S>5315</S> is even as a weaned child<S>1580</S>. <sup>myself: Heb. my soul</sup>
KJV.3: Let Israel<S>3478</S> hope<S>3176</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S> from henceforth and for<S>5704</S> ever<S>5769</S>. <sup>henceforth: Heb. now</sup>

ESV.1: O LORD, my heart is not lifted up; my eyes are not raised too high; I do not occupy myself with things too great and too marvelous for me.
ESV.2: But I have calmed and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me.
ESV.3: O Israel, hope in the LORD from this time forth and forevermore.

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents, of David. O LORD, my heart is not proud, nor my eyes haughty; Nor do I involve myself in great matters, Or in things too difficult for me.
NASB.2: Surely I have composed and quieted my soul; Like a weaned child [rests] against his mother, My soul is like a weaned child within me.
NASB.3: O Israel, hope in the LORD From this time forth and forever.

NLT.1:
LORD, my heart is not proud;
my eyes are not haughty.
I don’t concern myself with matters too great
or too awesome for me to grasp.
NLT.2:
Instead, I have calmed and quieted myself,
like a weaned child who no longer cries for its mother’s milk.
Yes, like a weaned child is my soul within me.
NLT.3:
O Israel, put your hope in the LORD —
now and always.

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. By David. Yahweh, my heart isn’t haughty, nor my eyes lofty; nor do I concern myself with great matters, or things too wonderful for me.
WEB.2: Surely I have stilled and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with his mother, like a weaned child is my soul within me.
WEB.3: Israel, hope in Yahweh, from this time forward and forever more.

CSB17.1:
A song of ascents. Of David. [1] LORD, my heart is not proud;
my eyes are not haughty. ⓖ
I do not get involved with things
too great or too wondrous for me. ⓗ
CSB17.2:
Instead, I have calmed and quieted my soul
like a weaned child with its mother;
my soul is like a weaned child. ⓘ
CSB17.3:
Israel, put your hope in the LORD,
both now and forever. ⓙ

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of David. LORD, my heart is not proud, nor my eyes haughty;
Nor do I involve myself in great matters,
Or in things too difficult for me.
AMP.2:
Surely I have calmed and quieted my soul;
Like a weaned child [resting] with his mother,
My soul is like a weaned child within me [composed and freed from discontent].
AMP.3:
O Israel, hope in the LORD
From this time forth and forever.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
God, I'm not trying to rule the roost,
I don't want to be king of the mountain.
I haven't meddled where I have no business
or fantasized grandiose plans.
MSG.2:
I've kept my feet on the ground,
I've cultivated a quiet heart.
Like a baby content in its mother's arms,
my soul is a baby content.
MSG.3:
Wait, Israel, for God. Wait with hope.
Hope now; hope always!

NIV.1:
Psalm 131
A song of ascents. Of David.
My heart is not proud, O Lord,
my eyes are not haughty;
I do not concern myself with great matters
or things too wonderful for me.
NIV.2:
But I have stilled and quieted my soul;
like a weaned child with its mother,
like a weaned child is my soul within me.
NIV.3:
O Israel, put your hope in the Lord
both now and forevermore.

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, my heart is not lifted up,
my eyes are not raised too high;
I do not occupy myself with things
too great and too marvelous for me.
NRSVCE.2:
But I have calmed and quieted my soul,
like a weaned child with its mother;
my soul is like the weaned child that is with me.
NRSVCE.3:
O Israel, hope in the LORD
from this time on and forevermore.

NET.1: O LORD, my heart is not proud, nor do I have a haughty look. I do not have great aspirations, or concern myself with things that are beyond me.
NET.2: Indeed I am composed and quiet, like a young child carried by its mother; I am content like the young child I carry.
NET.3: O Israel, hope in the LORD now and forevermore!
